_comment: Procedural deadline rules for Singapore civil litigation. Based on Rules of Court 2021 (ROC 2021, S 914/2021, in force 1 April 2022) and Supreme Court Practice Directions 2021.
_disclaimer: This tool is for general reference only and does not constitute legal advice. Always verify deadlines against the current Rules of Court 2021 on sso.agc.gov.sg and current Practice Directions on judiciary.gov.sg. When in doubt, consult a qualified Singapore lawyer.

_dayCountingRules.triggerDay: The trigger date itself is NOT counted. The period runs from the day after the trigger event (Interpretation Act s 50(a)).
_dayCountingRules.shortPeriods: For periods of 5 days or fewer (O 2 r 3, ROC 2021): Saturdays, Sundays, and public holidays are EXCLUDED from the count (effectively working days only).
_dayCountingRules.vacationExclusion: For proceedings in the Supreme Court: where the period is 14 days or fewer (O 2 r 4, ROC 2021), court vacation days are also EXCLUDED from the count (the clock pauses during vacation). For periods longer than 14 days, vacation days ARE counted — but if the deadline falls on a vacation day, it extends to the first Registry-open day after vacation.
_dayCountingRules.lastDayRule: If the last day of ANY period falls on a Saturday, Sunday, public holiday, or (for SC) a court vacation day, the deadline is extended to the next Registry-open day.

triggers.label: Service of Writ of Summons (without Statement of Claim)

deadlines.label: Enter Memorandum of Appearance
deadlines.note: Defendant served in Singapore must enter appearance within 8 days after service of Writ. For defendants served outside Singapore, see the leave to serve out order for the time allowed.

triggers.label: Service of Writ with Statement of Claim (defendant in Singapore)

deadlines.label: Enter Memorandum of Appearance
deadlines.note: Defendant must enter appearance within 8 days after service.

deadlines.label: File and Serve Defence
deadlines.note: Where the Statement of Claim is endorsed on or served with the Writ, the defendant must file and serve Defence within 14 days after service of the Writ.

triggers.label: Service of Writ of Summons (defendant outside Singapore)

deadlines.label: Enter Memorandum of Appearance
deadlines.note: Defendant served outside Singapore must enter appearance within 21 days, or such other time as fixed in the order granting leave to serve out of jurisdiction.

deadlines.label: File and Serve Defence
deadlines.note: Defendant served outside Singapore must file and serve Defence within 21 days after service, or as ordered.

triggers.label: Service of Statement of Claim (served separately, after Writ)

deadlines.label: File and Serve Defence
deadlines.note: Defendant (who has already entered appearance) must file and serve Defence within 14 days after service of the Statement of Claim.

triggers.label: Service of Defence

deadlines.label: File and Serve Reply (if any)
deadlines.note: If the plaintiff wishes to file a Reply, it must be filed and served within 14 days after service of the Defence. A Reply is not mandatory.

triggers.label: Service of Defence and Counterclaim

deadlines.label: File and Serve Reply to Defence (if any)
deadlines.note: Reply to the Defence (if any) must be filed within 14 days. Not mandatory.

deadlines.label: File and Serve Defence to Counterclaim
deadlines.note: The plaintiff must file and serve a Defence to Counterclaim within 14 days after service of the Defence and Counterclaim.

triggers.label: Service of Originating Application (on Respondent)

deadlines.label: File and Serve Respondent's Affidavit
deadlines.note: Respondent must file and serve their affidavit(s) in reply within 21 days after service of the Originating Application (and supporting affidavit).

triggers.label: Service of Respondent's Affidavit (Originating Application)

deadlines.label: File and Serve Applicant's Reply Affidavit (if any)
deadlines.note: Applicant's reply affidavit (if any) must be filed within 14 days of service of the respondent's affidavit.

triggers.label: Service of List of Documents

deadlines.label: Allow Inspection of Documents
deadlines.note: The party serving the List of Documents must allow inspection of the listed documents within 7 days of serving the List. Note: for ≤5 day periods, weekends/PHs are excluded; this is a 7-day period so calendar days apply but vacation exclusion applies for SC (≤14 days).

triggers.label: Service of Summons / Interlocutory Application

deadlines.label: File and Serve Respondent's Affidavit
deadlines.note: For interlocutory applications, the responding party typically has 14 days to file and serve their affidavit(s) in reply after service of the summons and supporting affidavit. Confirm with any specific court directions.

triggers.label: Service of Respondent's Affidavit (Interlocutory Application)

deadlines.label: File and Serve Applicant's Reply Affidavit
deadlines.note: Applicant's reply affidavit is typically due 7 days after service of the respondent's affidavit. The 7-day period is ≤14 days, so court vacation days are excluded from the count for Supreme Court matters.

triggers.label: Date of Judgment / Order — High Court (General Division)
triggers.reference: O 19 r 4, ROC 2021; Supreme Court of Judicature Act

deadlines.label: Request Further Arguments (where required before appealing)
deadlines.note: For certain interlocutory orders from the General Division, a party must request further arguments from the judge within 14 days of the order before being entitled to appeal. Time for appeal runs from when the judge certifies no further arguments are required (or 14 days after the request if no response). NOT required for all orders — check if applicable.

deadlines.label: File Notice of Appeal to Court of Appeal
deadlines.note: Notice of Appeal to the Court of Appeal must be filed within 1 month (treated as 30 days for calculation purposes) of the judgment or order. If further arguments are required first, time runs from the judge's certificate or deemed refusal.

triggers.label: Date of Judgment / Order — State Courts (Interlocutory, for Appeal to HC)
triggers.reference: O 19 r 8, ROC 2021; State Courts Act

deadlines.label: File Notice of Appeal to General Division (High Court)
deadlines.reference: O 19 r 8(1), ROC 2021; State Courts Act
deadlines.note: For interlocutory orders from State Courts, Notice of Appeal to the High Court (General Division) must be filed within 14 days of the judgment or order.

triggers.label: Date of Final Judgment / Order — State Courts (for Appeal to HC)
triggers.reference: O 19 r 8, ROC 2021; State Courts Act

deadlines.label: File Notice of Appeal to General Division (High Court)
deadlines.reference: O 19 r 8(1), ROC 2021; State Courts Act
deadlines.note: For final judgments from State Courts, Notice of Appeal to the High Court must be filed within 28 days of the judgment or order.

triggers.label: Filing of Notice of Appeal (to Court of Appeal)

deadlines.label: File Record of Appeal
deadlines.note: The Appellant must file the Record of Appeal within 60 days of filing the Notice of Appeal.

deadlines.label: File Appellant's Case
deadlines.note: Appellant's Case is filed simultaneously with the Record of Appeal, within 60 days of filing the Notice of Appeal.

triggers.label: Service of Record of Appeal and Appellant's Case

deadlines.label: File Respondent's Case
deadlines.note: Respondent's Case must be filed within 42 days of service of the Record of Appeal and Appellant's Case.

triggers.label: Service of Respondent's Case

deadlines.label: File Appellant's Reply (if any)
deadlines.note: Appellant's Reply (if any) must be filed within 14 days of service of the Respondent's Case.

triggers.label: Service of Opponent's Notice of Appeal (Cross-Appeal)

deadlines.label: File Cross-Appeal (Notice of Appeal / Respondent's Notice)
deadlines.note: A cross-appeal must be filed within the time allowed for a Notice of Appeal, or within 14 days of service of the other party's Notice of Appeal, whichever is later. Run this from the date of service of the opponent's Notice of Appeal.

triggers.label: Date of Registrar's Decision / Order

deadlines.label: File Notice of Appeal from Registrar to Judge in Chambers
deadlines.note: An appeal from a Registrar's decision to a Judge in Chambers (review) must be filed within 14 days of the Registrar's decision.

triggers.label: Date of Issue of Writ of Summons

deadlines.label: Serve Writ (validity period — Singapore service)
deadlines.note: A Writ is valid for 3 months (90 days) from date of issue for service in Singapore. Apply to renew (in further 3-month increments) before expiry if service has not been effected.

deadlines.label: Serve Writ (validity period — service outside Singapore)
deadlines.note: Where leave has been granted to serve outside Singapore, the Writ is valid for 6 months (180 days) from date of issue.

triggers.label: Date of Conclusion of Proceedings (for Costs Taxation)

deadlines.label: File Bill of Costs for Taxation
deadlines.note: A Bill of Costs for taxation must be filed within 6 months (180 days) of the conclusion of the proceedings (or as ordered). Proceedings are deemed concluded on the date of final judgment, settlement, or discontinuance.

triggers.label: Date of Receipt of Arbitral Award
triggers.reference: International Arbitration Act (Cap 143A) s 24; Arbitration Act (Cap 10) s 48

deadlines.label: File Application to Set Aside Arbitral Award
deadlines.reference: International Arbitration Act s 24(1); Arbitration Act s 48(2)
deadlines.note: Application to the High Court to set aside an arbitral award must be made within 3 months (90 days) of receipt of the award (or, if a request for correction/interpretation was made, from the date that request was disposed of). This time limit is strict and cannot be extended by agreement.

triggers.label: Service of Writ — State Courts (District Court / Magistrates' Court)

deadlines.label: Enter Memorandum of Appearance
deadlines.note: Defendant served in Singapore must enter appearance within 8 days after service of Writ.

deadlines.label: File and Serve Defence
deadlines.note: Defendant must file and serve Defence within 14 days after service of Writ (with Statement of Claim). Note: State Courts do not have the same vacation exclusion rules as the Supreme Court.
